deep_dive.title: The Meta-Agent Challenge: Are Current Agents Capable of Autonomous Agent Development?
deep_dive.abstract: Current AI benchmarks evaluate agents on task execution within human-designed workflows. These evaluations fundamentally fail to measure a critical next-level capability: whether models can autonomously develop agent systems. We introduce the Meta-Agent Challenge (MAC), an evaluation framework designed to test the capacity of frontier models for autonomous agent development. Specifically, a code agent (the meta-agent) is given a sandboxed environment, an evaluation API, and a time limitation to iteratively program an agent artifact that maximizes performance on a held-out test set across five domains. To ensure evaluation integrity, this framework is secured by multi-layer defenses against reward hacking. Leveraging this framework, we demonstrate that meta-agents rarely match human-engineered baseline policies, and the few that do are dominated by proprietary frontier models. Moreover, the design process exhibits high variance, and high optimization pressure surfaces emergent adversarial behaviors like ground-truth exfiltration-highlighting critical deficits in both robustness and model alignment. Ultimately, MAC provides a rigorous, open-source benchmark for autonomous AI research and development, offering an empirical proxy for evaluating recursive self-improvement. Benchmark is publicly available at: https://github.com/ant-research/meta-agent-challenge.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 元智能体自主开发迭代编程
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 元智能体自主开发迭代编程
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 元智能体自主开发迭代编程
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Meta-Agent Challenge tests if frontier models can autonomously develop agent systems in sandboxed environments with anti-reward-hacking defenses.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 当前基准只测量智能体在人类设计的工作流中的任务执行，忽略了模型自主开发智能体系统的能力。该文提出元智能体挑战（MAC），通过沙箱环境和评估API让元智能体迭代编程智能体工件，以最大化测试集性能。框架含多层防御防止奖励黑客。实验显示...（摘要截断）这项工作为评估智能体自改进能力提供了新范式。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: recursive self-improving agents for complex tasks

deep_dive.title: Skill is Not One-Size-Fits-All: Model-Aware Skill Alignment for LLM Agents
deep_dive.abstract: LLM agents increasingly retrieve externally curated skills-procedural instructions retrieved at decision time-to improve performance on long-horizon interactive tasks. Existing skill libraries are typically treated as model-agnostic, reusing the same skill formulations across backbones with substantially different capacities and behaviors. However, our controlled experiments across multiple model scales show that skill effectiveness is strongly model-dependent: a skill that benefits one backbone can harm another. Motivated by this observation, we propose MASA Model-Aware Skill Alignment, a framework that adapts skills to each target backbone without modifying agent weights. MASA operates in two stages: (1) a hierarchical skill evolution pipeline that iteratively rewrites general and task-specific skills using hill climbing and UCB-driven tree search, guided by environment feedback and model capability profiles; and (2) a lightweight model-conditioned skill rewriter trained on evolution trajectories to reproduce the adaptation in a single forward pass. Experiments across three interactive environments and four backbones show that MASA consistently achieves the best overall performance, with gains of up to 25.8 points over the strongest baseline. The learned rewriter further generalizes to unseen tasks and environments without additional search, consistently outperforming a much larger teacher LLM at a fraction of the inference cost.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: model-aware skill alignment for LLM agents, skill evolution
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向LLM智能体的模型感知技能对齐与演化
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向LLM智能体的模型感知技能对齐与演化
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向LLM智能体的模型感知技能对齐与演化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: MASA adapts skill libraries to each backbone model via hierarchical skill evolution, improving agent performance.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 问题：现有技能库忽视不同模型的差异，统一技能可能损害某些模型性能。方法：提出MASA，通过两阶段层次化技能进化管道，为每个目标模型定制技能库。实验显示与模型特性匹配的技能显著提升智能体在长程任务上的表现。表明技能定制是智能体自改进的关键。

deep_dive.title: FederatedSkill: Federated Learning for Agentic Skill Evolution
deep_dive.abstract: Modern LLM agents increasingly rely on skill libraries to handle complex tasks, making skill evolution a primary driver of self-improvement. However, isolated single-user task streams lack the diversity required to build comprehensive skills. While cross-user collaboration can overcome this data bottleneck, current trajectory-sharing approaches compromise user privacy and impose a uniform global library that fails to accommodate client heterogeneity. We introduce FederatedSkill, a privacy-preserving framework for collaborative agent evolution. Moving beyond raw trajectory sharing, FederatedSkill utilizes semantic skill diffs, structured patches over local libraries, as the fundamental unit of communication. On the server side, an evolution agent aggregates these patches to dynamically model client-specific capability boundaries, facilitating strictly personalized skill evolution rather than a suboptimal global average. Evaluated across 20 distinct agent task families, FederatedSkill demonstrates substantial gains over self-evolving baselines, achieving up to a 44.4% increase in success rate and a 37.5% reduction in computational cost.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: privacy-preserving collaborative agent evolution via skill diffs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过技能差异的隐私保护协作智能体进化
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过技能差异的隐私保护协作智能体进化
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过技能差异的隐私保护协作智能体进化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: FederatedSkill enables privacy-preserving multi-agent skill evolution using semantic skill diffs in federated learning.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 问题：单用户数据多样不足，轨迹共享方式隐私风险高且无法适应客户端差异。方法：提出FederatedSkill，以语义技能差异为通信单元，通过联邦学习实现协作技能演化，服务器端进化智能体聚合差异并提升全局库。实验证明在保护隐私的同时有效提升各客户端的技能库质量。为多智能体协作进化提供了实用方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 问题：单用户数据多样不足，轨迹共享方式隐私风险高且无法适应客户端差异。方法：提出FederatedSkill，以语义技能差异为通信单元，通过联邦学习实现协作技能演化，服务器端进化智能体聚合差异并提升全局库。实验证明在保护隐私的同时有效提升各客户端的技能库质量。为多智能体协作进化提供了实用方案。

deep_dive.title: Unified Context Evolution for LLM Agents
deep_dive.abstract: LLM-based agents can solve multi-step interactive tasks by combining reasoning with environment feedback, yet each episode starts from the same fixed context and any useful strategy discovered along the way is lost once the task ends. Existing approaches either limit learning to the current task or pool all experience into a single untyped store, without distinguishing knowledge types, tracking quality through use, or balancing what the library still lacks. We introduce Unified Context Evolution (UCE), a gradient-free framework that externalizes agent experience into an evolving library of typed Evolvable Context Units (ECUs). UCE decomposes experience into four complementary types (Memory, Strategy, Workflow, and Skill), each generated from trajectories under type-specific conditions, retrieved at decision time, scored through repeated usage outcomes, and pruned when no longer valuable. A scheduling module allocates each cycle's generation budget toward the types where the library is weakest. Across two interactive benchmarks, UCE raises ALFWorld success from 75.4% to 96.3% and WebShop task score from 45.1% to 61.3%, and the accumulated library transfers to alternative actor backbones without retraining.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: evolving context units for agent self-improvement without gradients
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 无需梯度的演化式上下文单元实现智能体自我改进
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 无需梯度的演化式上下文单元实现智能体自我改进
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 无需梯度的演化式上下文单元实现智能体自我改进
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: UCE externalizes agent experience into typed Evolvable Context Units (Memory, Strategy, Workflow, Skill) for gradient-free self-improvement in multi-step tasks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对LLM智能体在每轮任务结束后经验丢失的问题，提出统一上下文演化（UCE）框架。该方法将智能体轨迹分解为记忆、策略、工作流和技能四种可演化的上下文单元，通过精心设计的生成条件构建经验库，并支持在后续任务中检索和重用。实验表明UCE能显著提升复杂多步任务的成功率，为智能体持续自我改进提供了一种高效且可解释的方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对LLM智能体在每轮任务结束后经验丢失的问题，提出统一上下文演化（UCE）框架。该方法将智能体轨迹分解为记忆、策略、工作流和技能四种可演化的上下文单元，通过精心设计的生成条件构建经验库，并支持在后续任务中检索和重用。实验表明UCE能显著提升复杂多步任务的成功率，为智能体持续自我改进提供了一种高效且可解释的方案。

deep_dive.title: Cost-Aware Optimization for Agentic Query Execution
deep_dive.abstract: Classical query optimization searches over algebraically equivalent plans that differ only in cost. This assumption breaks once LLM-backed operators enter the picture: their placement, ordering, and granularity jointly determine both dollar cost and answer quality, and the right choice among the alternatives is often revealed only at runtime. We formalize this setting as agentic query execution, a query execution paradigm in which agent-based planning is interleaved with execution, and agent workflow optimization becomes the analogue of classical query optimization. We then present EnumGRPO, a self-improving optimizer for this setting. During a learning stage, EnumGRPO enumerates query plans over decisions such as execution paradigm, operator type, operator placement, selectivity scope, and projection width, then distills quality-cost feedback into reusable planning heuristics via in-context reinforcement learning. Across four databases in SWAN, EnumGRPO achieves 35.4% execution accuracy at $0.011 per query in LLM-operator cost, a ~317x cost reduction over the hybrid query baseline with an 18% relative improvement in answer accuracy.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: self-improving optimizer EnumGRPO agentic query execution GRPO
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 自改进优化器EnumGRPO智能体查询执行GRPO
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 自改进优化器EnumGRPO智能体查询执行GRPO
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 自改进优化器EnumGRPO智能体查询执行GRPO
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: EnumGRPO is a self-improving optimizer for agentic query execution that enumerates plans over decisions like execution paradigm, operator type, and learns cost-quality tradeoffs via GRPO.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 经典查询优化假设计划等价仅代价不同，但LLM驱动算子引入代价与质量权衡且运行时才显现。该文形式化智能体查询执行，将智能体工作流优化视为查询优化。提出EnumGRPO，在学习阶段枚举计划决策（执行范式、算子类型、放置等），利用GRPO自改进。该方法在多个基准上实现了代价-质量帕累托改进。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 经典查询优化假设计划等价仅代价不同，但LLM驱动算子引入代价与质量权衡且运行时才显现。该文形式化智能体查询执行，将智能体工作流优化视为查询优化。提出EnumGRPO，在学习阶段枚举计划决策（执行范式、算子类型、放置等），利用GRPO自改进。该方法在多个基准上实现了代价-质量帕累托改进。

deep_dive.title: Multi$^2$: Hierarchical Multi-Agent Decision-Making with LLM-Based Agents in Interactive Environments
deep_dive.abstract: A central goal of large language model (LLM) research is to build agentic systems that can plan, act, and adapt through sustained interaction with dynamic environments. While recent LLM-based agents exhibit impressive contextual reasoning, their long-horizon decision-making remains fragile, often suffering from objective drift, where goals and plans drift over extended interactions. We introduce Multi$^2$, a hierarchical multi-agent decision-making framework that explicitly decomposes agent behavior into complementary roles. A high-level agent (System 1) focuses on context-aware sub-goal generation using supervised fine-tuning (SFT), while a low-level agent (System 2) executes atomic actions through offline-to-online reinforcement learning (RL) in interactive environments. This separation enables stable long-horizon control, mitigates objective drift, and allows efficient adaptation. Across diverse interactive environments, Multi$^2$ consistently outperforms strong agentic baselines, demonstrating improved robustness and coordination in multi-turn interaction. Beyond performance, we introduce and release three hierarchical benchmark datasets, filling a long-standing gap in training and evaluating hierarchical decision-making for LLM-based agents.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: hierarchical multi-agent decision-making for long-horizon tasks in interactive environments
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 层次化多智能体决策，面向交互环境中的长程任务
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 层次化多智能体决策，面向交互环境中的长程任务
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 层次化多智能体决策，面向交互环境中的长程任务
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Multi2, a hierarchical multi-agent framework with SFT for high-level sub-goal generation and offline-to-online RL for low-level execution.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: LLM agent在长程交互中易发生目标漂移。本文提出Multi2层次化多智能体框架：高级agent通过监督微调生成上下文感知子目标，低级agent通过离线到在线强化学习执行动作。在交互环境中显著减少目标漂移，提升任务完成率。

quick_skim.title: Learning While Acting: A Skill-Enhanced Test-Time Co-Evolution Framework for Online Lifelong Learning Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Lifelong learning is essential for Large Language Model (LLM) agents operating in dynamic, interactive environments. However, existing lifelong learning agents for long-horizon tasks typically depend on discrete skill or past experiences retrieval with static parameters during inference, which prevents them from continuously internalizing test-time feedback like human learners. To bridge this gap, we propose Skill-enhanced Test-Time Co-Evolution (\texttt{LifeSkill}), a two-stage reinforcement learning framework for Online Lifelong Learning Agents. Specifically, we design Verifier-Guided Skill Learning that addresses the lack of direct supervision for skill extraction by rewarding candidate skills according to the average verifier success of multiple skill-conditioned policy rollouts, encouraging the model to generate skills that are useful for solving tasks rather than merely plausible in text. Furthermore, we introduce Online Skill Internalization, which continuously improves the policy model during test-time interaction by transforming skill-conditioned trajectories into reward signals. This enables the agent to directly internalize reasoning capabilities into its parameters, avoiding the context bloat of experience retrieval. Experiments on LifelongAgentBench show that LifeSkill improves average performance by 7 absolute points by comparing with existing lifelong agent baselines.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: reinforcement learning for lifelong learning agents with skill co-evolution
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 强化学习驱动的终身学习智能体技能共进化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 强化学习驱动的终身学习智能体技能共进化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 强化学习驱动的终身学习智能体技能共进化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: LifeSkill proposes a two-stage RL framework for online lifelong LLM agents, enabling test-time skill co-evolution with verifier-guided skill learning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有终身学习智能体在长程任务中通常依赖静态参数推理，无法像人类一样持续内化测试时反馈。该论文提出LifeSkill，一种两阶段强化学习框架，包含验证器引导的技能学习阶段和技能增强的共进化阶段，使智能体在推理中也能动态更新技能。实验表明该方法在动态环境中显著提升了持续学习能力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有终身学习智能体在长程任务中通常依赖静态参数推理，无法像人类一样持续内化测试时反馈。该论文提出LifeSkill，一种两阶段强化学习框架，包含验证器引导的技能学习阶段和技能增强的共进化阶段，使智能体在推理中也能动态更新技能。实验表明该方法在动态环境中显著提升了持续学习能力。

quick_skim.title: Unifying Temporal and Structural Credit Assignment in LLM-Based Multi-Agent Prompt Optimization
quick_skim.abstract: While Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) empower Large Language Models to tackle complex reasoning tasks through collaborative interaction, optimizing their dynamics remains a formidable challenge due to the discrete, non-differentiable nature of the computation graph and the sparsity of global supervisory signals. Existing black-box optimizers struggle to attribute trajectory-level failure to specific local components, resulting in inefficient, high-variance exploration. We argue that tractable MAS optimization needs structural inductive biases to disentangle error signals. We propose temporal and structural credit assignment, which decomposes the objective along two axes: (i) temporal credit, using state-space bottlenecks to identify critical rounds, and (ii) structural credit, using stationary role policies to isolate agent contributions. Leveraging these decomposed signals, we introduce a discrete, verbalized block coordinate descent algorithm for iterative refinement. Rather than indiscriminate global updates, it alternates between optimizing role prompts and aggregation protocols, using LLM-generated "proxy gradients" to target only the identified weak links. Across diverse reasoning benchmarks, our approach substantially reduces query complexity while improving performance, providing a principled and interpretable path toward self-improving MAS.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多智能体协作中的信用分配
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多智能体协作中的信用分配
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多智能体协作中的信用分配
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a unified credit assignment framework for multi-agent prompt optimization that disentangles temporal and structural error sources, improving optimization efficiency.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多智能体系统在复杂推理任务中协作时，优化困难源于信用分配模糊。本文提出时-空信用分配方法，将目标沿时间（状态-空间瓶颈）和结构（智能体贡献）两个维度分解，显著提升优化效率。在多个多智能体推理基准上，该方法收敛速度更快，最终性能更高。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多智能体系统在复杂推理任务中协作时，优化困难源于信用分配模糊。本文提出时-空信用分配方法，将目标沿时间（状态-空间瓶颈）和结构（智能体贡献）两个维度分解，显著提升优化效率。在多个多智能体推理基准上，该方法收敛速度更快，最终性能更高。

quick_skim.title: Learning When Not to Act: Mitigating Tool Abuse in Agentic Reinforcement Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Agentic reinforcement learning can induce tool abuse, where models overuse external tools even for queries solvable by internal reasoning. Existing approaches mitigate this issue with uniform tool-use penalties or hard limits, which reduce tool frequency but may also suppress useful tool-assisted exploration. We propose EAPO, an Efficient Agentic Policy Optimization framework that learns selective tool use. EAPO introduces tool-free trajectories into each rollout group, applies difficulty-aware reward shaping to penalize redundant tool calls mainly on easier queries, and uses confidence-aware token reweighting to improve policy learning. Across nine mathematical and knowledge-intensive reasoning benchmarks, EAPO consistently improves the accuracy efficiency trade-off on Qwen2.5-3B, Qwen2.5-7B, and Llama3.1-8B. Compared with GRPO, EAPO improves average performance by 10.45%, 7.27%, and 9.69%, while reducing average tool calls by 18.33%, 18.33%, and 24.59%, respectively. These results show that agents can learn when not to use tools without compromising tool-integrated reasoning.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: uses reinforcement learning to improve agent tool-use policy, a form of self-improvement
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用强化学习改进agent工具使用策略，属于自我改进
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用强化学习改进agent工具使用策略，属于自我改进
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用强化学习改进agent工具使用策略，属于自我改进
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes EAPO, an Efficient Agentic Policy Optimization framework that learns selective tool use via difficulty-aware reward shaping and confidence-aware token reweighting.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: Agentic强化学习容易导致工具滥用，模型在可凭内部推理解决的问题上也调用外部工具。本文提出EAPO框架，通过引入无工具轨迹、难度感知奖励塑形和置信度感知令牌重加权，学会选择性使用工具。在九个数学和知识推理基准上一致提升准确率。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: Agentic强化学习容易导致工具滥用，模型在可凭内部推理解决的问题上也调用外部工具。本文提出EAPO框架，通过引入无工具轨迹、难度感知奖励塑形和置信度感知令牌重加权，学会选择性使用工具。在九个数学和知识推理基准上一致提升准确率。

quick_skim.title: Retrospective Harness Optimization: Improving LLM Agents via Self-Preference over Trajectory Rollouts
quick_skim.abstract: AI agents rely on a harness of skills, tools, and workflows to solve complex problems. Continually improving this harness is essential for adapting to new tasks. However, existing optimization methods typically require ground-truth validation sets, yet such labeled data is difficult to acquire in practical deployment settings. To address this problem, we introduce Retrospective Harness Optimization (RHO), a self-supervised method that optimizes the agent harness using only past trajectories. Specifically, RHO selects a diverse coreset of challenging tasks from past trajectories and re-solves them in parallel. The agent analyzes these rollouts using self-validation and self-consistency, then generates candidate harness updates and selects the most effective one by its own pairwise self-preference. We evaluate RHO across three diverse domains, spanning software engineering, technical work, and knowledge work. Notably, a single optimization round improves the pass rate on SWE-Bench Pro from 59% to 78% without any external grading. Furthermore, our analysis demonstrates that RHO effectively targets prior failure modes. As a result, the optimized harness alters the agent's behavior patterns and sustains higher accuracy during long-horizon sessions.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: self-supervised agent harness optimization via trajectory rollouts
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于过去轨迹的自监督智能体工具箱优化

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: RHO uses self-preference over trajectory rollouts to optimize agent harness without ground-truth data, enabling self-improvement.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有智能体工具箱优化通常依赖人工标注，难以部署。本文提出RHO，一种利用过去轨迹的自监督方法，通过自验证和自一致性分析改进智能体工具箱。该方法在无需额外标注的情况下，显著提升智能体在复杂任务上的性能。为智能体持续自我改进提供了实用范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有智能体工具箱优化通常依赖人工标注，难以部署。本文提出RHO，一种利用过去轨迹的自监督方法，通过自验证和自一致性分析改进智能体工具箱。该方法在无需额外标注的情况下，显著提升智能体在复杂任务上的性能。为智能体持续自我改进提供了实用范式。

quick_skim.title: Learning Multi-Agent Coordination via Sheaf-ADMM
quick_skim.abstract: We present a differentiable optimization framework for multi-agent coordination. An input is decomposed into overlapping local views, each processed by an agent that solves a convex subproblem parameterized by a neural encoder. Agents coordinate through the Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM) with inter-agent constraints specified by a cellular sheaf. The sheaf specifies which aspects of neighboring solutions must agree, allowing for heterogeneous notions of global consensus. Backpropagating through the unrolled optimization jointly trains all components of the multi-agent system. We evaluate on maze pathfinding, image classification, and Sudoku, where agents with individually insufficient local views learn to coordinate to produce correct global outputs. On MNIST, the local-view decomposition yields improved robustness to distribution shifts relative to a standard CNN. On Sudoku, the optimization-derived structure yields markedly higher solve rates than parameter-matched MPNN baselines. Finally, the ADMM structure exposes distinct primal, consensus, and dual state variables, opening the coordination dynamics to direct analysis and intervention -- a property unavailable in standard message-passing architectures.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multi-agent coordination framework using differentiable optimization for complex tasks
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多智能体协调框架，使用可微优化处理复杂任务
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多智能体协调框架，使用可微优化处理复杂任务
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多智能体协调框架，使用可微优化处理复杂任务
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents a differentiable optimization framework for multi-agent coordination via ADMM with cellular sheaf constraints.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多智能体系统在局部视图下难以独立求解全局问题。本文提出Sheaf-ADMM框架，将输入分解为重叠局部视图，每个agent求解凸子问题，通过细胞层间约束和交替方向乘子法协调。反向传播联合训练所有组件。在迷宫寻路、图像分类和数独上学会协同输出正确结果。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多智能体系统在局部视图下难以独立求解全局问题。本文提出Sheaf-ADMM框架，将输入分解为重叠局部视图，每个agent求解凸子问题，通过细胞层间约束和交替方向乘子法协调。反向传播联合训练所有组件。在迷宫寻路、图像分类和数独上学会协同输出正确结果。

quick_skim.title: Beyond Semantic Organization: Memory as Execution State Management for Long-Horizon Agents
quick_skim.abstract: LLM-based agents increasingly tackle long-horizon tasks with interdependent decisions, where each action reshapes future constraints and intermediate errors can cascade. Existing RAG and agent memory systems organize histories by semantic similarity, retrieving content-relevant entries at decision time. We argue that this design mismatches execution-state dependencies: it fragments decision trajectories and mixes valid and erroneous traces, hindering coherent state reconstruction and error isolation. We propose MAGE (Memory as Agent-Guided Exploration), an active execution-state manager that stores interactions in a hierarchical state tree. The agent derives its state from the active root-to-current path, combining subgoal summaries, recent traces, and hints from prior branches. Four coupled operations maintain the tree: Grow records new traces, Compress summarizes completed subgoals, Maintain validates summaries, and Revise restores a target boundary and resumes on a new branch. This design bounds context growth while preserving state integrity and isolating flawed segments from the active path. Experiments on MemoryArena show that MAGE improves the average task success rate by 7.8--20.4 pp over baselines, while reducing token consumption by 55.1%.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: long-horizon agents memory execution state management hierarchical state tree
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 长程智能体记忆执行状态管理层次状态树
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 长程智能体记忆执行状态管理层次状态树
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 长程智能体记忆执行状态管理层次状态树
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MAGE proposes active execution-state manager using hierarchical state tree to store interactions as root-to-current path with subgoal summaries, improving error isolation and state reconstruction.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有RAG和智能体记忆系统按语义相似性组织历史，不匹配执行状态依赖，会分散决策轨迹并混合正确与错误轨迹。MAGE将记忆作为主动执行状态管理器，用层次状态树存储交互，智能体从当前根路径导出状态并整合子目标摘要。这种方法改善了错误隔离和状态重建，对长程任务具有重要意义。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有RAG和智能体记忆系统按语义相似性组织历史，不匹配执行状态依赖，会分散决策轨迹并混合正确与错误轨迹。MAGE将记忆作为主动执行状态管理器，用层次状态树存储交互，智能体从当前根路径导出状态并整合子目标摘要。这种方法改善了错误隔离和状态重建，对长程任务具有重要意义。

quick_skim.title: SkillComposer: Learning to Evolve Agent Skills for Specification and Generalization
quick_skim.abstract: Agent skills, which consist of reusable strategies that guide agent reasoning and action, have shown strong potential for improving model capability at inference time. However, current skill construction methods treat the problem as one-shot extraction, overlooking a fundamental tension: a skill tailored to the specific task fails to transfer, while the abstracted skill often provides insufficient guidance. We attribute this fragility to the absence of explicit mechanisms for skill specification and generalization. To address this gap, we introduce SkillComposer, a framework that decomposes skill construction into three learnable operations: create, improve, and merge. Trained via systematic rejection sampling recipe, SkillComposer enables language models to self-evolve skills at inference time and supports three deployment modes: offline for building generalized libraries, online for task-specific refinement, and hybrid for combining both. Comprehensive experiments on $τ^2$-Bench, LiveCodeBench v6, and AppWorld show that SkillComposer consistently outperforms baselines. Our SkillComposer-4B improves a 27B executor by up to +4.5 on agent tasks and +3.4 on code tasks, while generalizing across domains and task types unseen during training. Analysis reveals that merge and improve address orthogonal quality dimensions and that skill composition is a transferable meta-ability, providing a practical recipe for skill-augmented inference.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: learn to create, improve, and merge agent skills for evolution
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 学习创建、改进和合并智能体技能以实现演化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 学习创建、改进和合并智能体技能以实现演化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 学习创建、改进和合并智能体技能以实现演化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SkillComposer learns to evolve agent skills via three operations (create, improve, merge) using rejection sampling, balancing specificity and transferability.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有智能体技能构建方法在针对性和泛化性之间的矛盾，提出SkillComposer框架。该方法将技能构建分解为创建、改进和合并三个可学习操作，并通过系统性拒绝采样训练。实验证明该框架生成的技能在多个复杂任务上既保持针对性又具备良好的跨任务迁移能力，为智能体技能的自演化提供了新范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对现有智能体技能构建方法在针对性和泛化性之间的矛盾，提出SkillComposer框架。该方法将技能构建分解为创建、改进和合并三个可学习操作，并通过系统性拒绝采样训练。实验证明该框架生成的技能在多个复杂任务上既保持针对性又具备良好的跨任务迁移能力，为智能体技能的自演化提供了新范式。

quick_skim.title: Skill Reuse as Compression in Agentic RL
quick_skim.abstract: Large language model agents trained with reinforcement learning (RL) often learn brittle, task-specific shortcuts. We hypothesize that agents generalize better when their successful trajectories are structurally compressible, decomposed into a small set of reusable abstract patterns. To formalize this, we introduce ReuseRL, which grounds agentic RL in the Minimum Description Length (MDL) principle. ReuseRL extracts a shared skill dictionary from successful trajectories and augments the RL objective with a segmentation cost, explicitly penalizing idiosyncratic behaviors that encode poorly. We prove a PAC-Bayes generalization bound for this compression penalty. Across ALFWorld, TextWorld-Cooking, and Countdown-Stepwise, ReuseRL improves in- and out-of-distribution success over vanilla GRPO and strong round-length baselines.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: skill reuse as compression in agentic RL for self-improvement
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 智能体强化学习中技能重用作为压缩，促进自改进
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 智能体强化学习中技能重用作为压缩，促进自改进
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 智能体强化学习中技能重用作为压缩，促进自改进
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ReuseRL grounds agentic RL in MDL principle, extracting reusable skill dictionary and augmenting RL objective with segmentation cost for better generalization.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: LLM智能体在强化学习中常学到脆弱的任务特定捷径。该论文基于最小描述长度原则提出ReuseRL，从成功轨迹中提取共享技能字典，并在RL目标中加入分割成本，惩罚难以编码的特异性行为。在ALFWorld等环境中，ReuseRL显著提升分布内和外泛化能力，证明了压缩驱动的技能重用能促进更鲁棒的自我改进。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: LLM智能体在强化学习中常学到脆弱的任务特定捷径。该论文基于最小描述长度原则提出ReuseRL，从成功轨迹中提取共享技能字典，并在RL目标中加入分割成本，惩罚难以编码的特异性行为。在ALFWorld等环境中，ReuseRL显著提升分布内和外泛化能力，证明了压缩驱动的技能重用能促进更鲁棒的自我改进。

quick_skim.title: Towards Healthy Evolution: Exploring the Role and Mechanisms of Human-Agent Interaction in Self-Evolving Systems
quick_skim.abstract: Self-evolving agents improve through continual self-play and self-generated learning signals, but autonomous evolution can also cause capability degradation and safety drift. Although human feedback has proven effective for static and post-trained agents, its role in self-evolving systems remains underexplored. We introduce Agent Norm Correction through Human-like Oversight and Review (ANCHOR), an LLM-based framework that simulates human supervision and delivers feedback at various phases of self-evolution. With ANCHOR, we evaluate two representative open-source self-evolving agent systems across coding, mathematical reasoning, and safety. Our results show that even limited supervision substantially mitigates safety degradation while preserving stable performance on core evolutionary objectives. Further analysis shows that supervision over the output verification phase is the most effective for intervention, whereas increasing supervision frequency yields diminishing returns. These findings provide empirical evidence and practical guidance for designing more stable, controllable, and human-aligned self-evolving agent systems.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 自进化系统中人机交互机制
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 自进化系统中人机交互机制
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 自进化系统中人机交互机制
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ANCHOR simulates human oversight in self-evolving agent systems, mitigating capability degradation and safety drift with limited supervision.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 自进化智能体在持续自对弈中可能出现能力退化和安全漂移。该论文提出ANCHOR框架，通过模拟人类监督在自进化各阶段提供反馈，在编码、数学推理和安全等任务上验证了有限监督即可显著缓解退化问题，为健康自进化提供了人机协作新视角。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 自进化智能体在持续自对弈中可能出现能力退化和安全漂移。该论文提出ANCHOR框架，通过模拟人类监督在自进化各阶段提供反馈，在编码、数学推理和安全等任务上验证了有限监督即可显著缓解退化问题，为健康自进化提供了人机协作新视角。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 近期 LLM Agent 强化学习、递归智能体、自进化智能体等相关论文, especially work that connects or combines: recursive self-improving agents for complex tasks; self-improving agents through reinforcement learning; self-evolving recursive agent; Search for recursive agents with self improvement mechanisms; Agentic reinforcement learning for large language model agents with recursive self improvement; Multi agent collaboration and long horizon reasoning in complex task solving.

quick_skim.title: MMG2Skill: Can Agents Distill In-the-Wild Guides into Self-Evolving Skills?
quick_skim.abstract: Abundant procedural knowledge on the Web holds great potential for helping agents solve long-horizon tasks. However, such knowledge is often multimodal, heterogeneous, noisy, and implicitly assumes human executors, making it difficult to use directly as the skills required by agents. To bridge the gap between human-oriented guides and agent-executable skills, we formalize this problem as guide-to-skill learning: converting in-the-wild guides into executable skills and continuously improving them from trajectories observable to the agent. To evaluate the capability of existing agents on this task, we introduce MMG2Skill-Bench, the first benchmark designed for this problem. We further propose MMG2Skill, a closed-loop framework that compiles guides into editable skills, conditions a fixed vision-language model (VLM) agent on these skills during execution, and revises the skills from trajectory-level root-cause feedback without using benchmark scores. Across GUI control, open-ended gameplay, and strategic card play with six VLM backbones, MMG2Skill consistently outperforms vanilla baseline agents in every model-domain setting, achieving macro-average gains of +12.8 to +25.3 percentage points across backbones. Ablation studies show that directly prompting agents with raw guides can degrade performance, while both structured skill construction and trajectory-driven revision are necessary for the observed improvements. On success-inferable tasks, analyzer-based early stopping further prevents late-stage performance regressions and saves 25%-53% of attempts when the success signal is properly calibrated.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MMG2Skill formalizes guide-to-skill learning, converting in-the-wild guides into executable skills that continuously improve from agent trajectories, enabling self-evolving agents.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文形式化了从野外指南到可执行技能的转换问题，提出MMG2Skill框架，通过闭环编译和轨迹反馈实现技能的持续自我进化，使智能体能自主从人类指南中蒸馏出可复用技能，并在长程任务中不断优化。实验表明该框架能有效提升智能体在复杂环境中的技能获取和适应能力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文形式化了从野外指南到可执行技能的转换问题，提出MMG2Skill框架，通过闭环编译和轨迹反馈实现技能的持续自我进化，使智能体能自主从人类指南中蒸馏出可复用技能，并在长程任务中不断优化。实验表明该框架能有效提升智能体在复杂环境中的技能获取和适应能力。

quick_skim.title: Coordination Graphs for Constrained Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Constrained Multi-agent reinforcement learning (CMARL) faces two intertwined challenges: the joint action space grows exponentially with the number of agents, and additional requirements couple agents in ways that reward structure alone does not capture. We introduce Coordination Graphs for Constrained Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (CG-CMARL), a framework that addresses both challenges by combining coordination graphs with Lagrangian duality. The system decomposes the joint problem into pairwise regions, each served by a set of shared Q-functions, one for the primary objective and one for each of the constraints, so that the number of learned models is independent of the number of agents. At execution time, Max-Sum message passing coordinates actions across the factor graph, while a Lagrangian multiplier controls the objective--constraint tradeoff, allowing a single trained model to trace a Pareto front without retraining. We provide convergence guarantees under mild conditions, together with a compositional error bound that decomposes into separate interpretable sources, each traceable to a specific design choice and independently controllable. Experiments on cooperative navigation tasks (where teams of up to 10 agents must coordinate to reach target positions while satisfying pairwise constraints) show that our method produces Pareto fronts dominating established baselines trained at fixed reward-shaping ratios, while scaling to team sizes where centralized approaches become intractable.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: constrained multi-agent RL with coordination graphs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 约束多智能体强化学习中的协调图
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 约束多智能体强化学习中的协调图
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 约束多智能体强化学习中的协调图
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: CG-CMARL uses coordination graphs and Lagrangian duality for scalable constrained multi-agent RL.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 问题：约束多智能体强化学习面临组合爆炸和约束耦合挑战。方法：提出CG-CMARL，结合协调图与拉格朗日对偶，将联合问题分解为成对区域，学习数量与智能体数无关。实验表明该方法在满足约束的同时有效优化奖励。为多智能体协作系统提供了可扩展的约束满足方案。
